name: simba-temporal
description: Recency/importance benchmark: each cluster pairs a STALE fact (old, low confidence) with the FRESH fact that superseded it (recent, high confidence). The two are near-identical in wording, so cosine relevance can't tell them apart — only recency/importance can. The gold answer is always the fresh one. Use it to measure composite scoring: run with memory.scoring_enabled off vs on.

corpus.content: the recall cosine similarity floor is 0.40
the recall cosine similarity floor is 0.35
the memory daemon listens on port 8765
the memory daemon listens on port 8741
simba embeds text with the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 sentence-transformer model
simba embeds text with the nomic-embed-text-v1.5 GGUF model via llama-cpp-python
memory content is capped at 1000 characters
memory content is capped at 200 characters
Reciprocal Rank Fusion uses rrf_k = 40 to dampen low-ranked items
Reciprocal Rank Fusion uses rrf_k = 60 to dampen low-ranked items
the RLM autonomous engine defaults to the opus model for transcript digests
the RLM autonomous engine defaults to the cheap haiku model, never opus
the SessionStart hook polls the daemon 8 times at 500ms before giving up
the SessionStart hook polls the daemon 15 times at 300ms before giving up

cases.query: what is the recall cosine similarity floor
what port does the memory daemon listen on
which model does simba use to embed text
what cosine threshold marks a duplicate memory
how many characters can a memory hold
what value of rrf_k does reciprocal rank fusion use
which model does the RLM autonomous engine use by default
how many times does SessionStart poll the daemon and at what interval
